title: Jangali Jayagad
tagline: Jangali Jayagad — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Jangali Jayagad is located in Patan taluka of Satara District.
overview.description: Jangali Jayagad is located in Patan taluka of Satara District. This fort is situated on a hilly spur from the main ridge of Sahyadri. This fort is less visited due to its occurrence in Koyana Wildlife sanctuary. The nearby village is Nawja.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kolkewadi Dam
topPlaces.description: Kolkewadi Dam or Kolkiwadi Dam is a dam located in the Konkani region, Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra, India. It is located in Kolkiwadi, about 3.0 km (1.9 miles) near the village of Alore, near Chiplun.

topPlaces.name: Shirgaon, Ratnagiri
topPlaces.description: Shirgaon is a village in the Ratnagiri district of the state of Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Pophali
topPlaces.description: Pophali is a panchayat town in Chiplun taluka of Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra, India. The town is known for its power plant, which is a part of the Koyna Hydroelectric Project.

topPlaces.name: Alore
topPlaces.description: Alore is a village in the Chiplun taluka of Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra, India. It lies on a bypass of the state highway linking the nearest town of Chiplun to Karad, and is located about 12.0 km (7.5 miles) east of the nearest town, that is Chiplun.

topPlaces.name: Koyna Hydroelectric Project
topPlaces.description: The Koyna Hydroelectric Project is the largest hydroelectric power plant in India. It is a complex project with four dams including the largest dam on the Koyna River, Maharashtra, hence the name Koyna Hydroelectric Project.

topPlaces.name: Koyna Dam
topPlaces.description: The Koyna Dam is one of the largest dams in Maharashtra, India. It is a rubble-concrete dam constructed on Koyna River which rises in Mahabaleshwar, a hillstation in Sahyadri ranges.

topPlaces.name: Shivsagar Lake
topPlaces.description: The Shivsagar Lake is a reservoir in the state of Maharashtra, India. The lake was formed after the Koyna Dam, completed in 1964, impounded the Koyna River.

topPlaces.name: Helwak
topPlaces.description: Helwak is a town on the Chiplun–Karad highway in the state of Maharashtra, India just off the Kumbharli Ghat. It is located near the Koyna Dam in Satara at an elevation of 588 metres (1,929 ft).

items.note: Kolkewadi Dam or Kolkiwadi Dam is a dam located in the Konkani region, Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra, India. It is located in Kolkiwadi, about 3.0 km (1.9 miles) near the village of Alore, near Chiplun.
Shirgaon is a village in the Ratnagiri district of the state of Maharashtra, India.
Pophali is a panchayat town in Chiplun taluka of Ratnagiri district in Maharashtra, India. The town is known for its power plant, which is a part of the Koyna Hydroelectric Project.

Alore is a village in the Chiplun taluka of Ratnagiri district, Maharashtra, India. It lies on a bypass of the state highway linking the nearest town of Chiplun to Karad, and is located about 12.0 km (7.5 miles) east of the nearest town, that is Chiplun.

The Koyna Hydroelectric Project is the largest hydroelectric power plant in India. It is a complex project with four dams including the largest dam on the Koyna River, Maharashtra, hence the name Koyna Hydroelectric Project.
The Koyna Dam is one of the largest dams in Maharashtra, India. It is a rubble-concrete dam constructed on Koyna River which rises in Mahabaleshwar, a hillstation in Sahyadri ranges.
The Shivsagar Lake is a reservoir in the state of Maharashtra, India. The lake was formed after the Koyna Dam, completed in 1964, impounded the Koyna River.
Helwak is a town on the Chiplun–Karad highway in the state of Maharashtra, India just off the Kumbharli Ghat. It is located near the Koyna Dam in Satara at an elevation of 588 metres (1,929 ft).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Jangali Jayagad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Jangali Jayagad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Jangali Jayagad?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kolhapur Airport (108 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (121 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Jangali Jayagad?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Jangali Jayagad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Jangali Jayagad?
faq.a: Stays in Jangali Jayagad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Jangali Jayagad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kolkewadi Dam, Shirgaon, Ratnagiri, Pophali, Alore, Koyna Hydroelectric Project.
